reviewTarget: Two-target files review: create.sh parent-packet description.json corruption bug (Target 1) + independent audit of shipped deriveStatus completion-evidence fix, phase 010, commits ea2bb09b7a/ca9bea9f78/b70a441388 (Target 2)

focus: correctness inventory pass - Target 1 create.sh parent description.json write path

findingDetails.title: Append-mode phase scaffolding overwrites the existing parent packet's description.json
findingDetails.claim: When --phase --phase-parent appends phases, FEATURE_DIR is the existing parent and the parent description generator is called with the new append request description, causing canonical parent metadata fields to be overwritten in folderPath/description.json.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the child generation call and found it uses _child_path, not the parent path; checked merge code and found canonical fields are overwritten, not preserved.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A separate metadata repair path could also rewrite description.json, but this direct create.sh path is sufficient to explain the reported parent metadata corruption.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if another runtime guard prevents the parent description generation block from running in append mode or rejects identity-changing saves for existing parents.

traceabilityChecks.spec_code: Target 1 code path confirmed; Target 2 REQ audit deferred by prompt scope.
traceabilityChecks.skill_agent: deep-review skill and review_core doctrine loaded before severity adjudication

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Target 1 is a non-trivial cross-script generated-metadata write path with known parent packet corruption symptoms.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These files contain the phase append state transition, the executed metadata generator, and the atomic description.json writer.
omittedHighRiskTargets: exhaustive repo-wide corruption classifier deferred to later Target 1 blast-radius iteration
Target 2 deriveStatus audit deferred by prompt instruction to inspect Target 1 only

searchLedger.hypothesis: Append mode reuses the existing parent path as FEATURE_DIR, so the parent description generation call writes child-append metadata to the parent description.json.

searchActions.result: APPEND_TO_EXISTING_PARENT sets FEATURE_DIR to the resolved parent and later calls generate-description.js on FEATURE_DIR with FEATURE_DESCRIPTION.
Explicit description mode derives specFolder and parentChain from basePath/folderPath and calls savePerFolderDescription.
savePerFolderDescription writes to folderPath/description.json through temp file and rename.

searchLedger.rationale: The direct call graph explains the reported parent metadata overwrite without live reproduction.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The child description generation call might be writing to the parent description.json.

searchActions.result: Child generation uses _child_path as the generator folder argument, so it targets child folders, not the parent.

searchLedger.rationale: The child loop is separate from the parent overwrite call and uses the child path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of generator arguments.

searchLedger.invariant: Existing parent description.json records should retain canonical specFolder ancestry and parentChain consistent with their actual path.

searchActions.result: Partial grep surfaced many metadata files but did not classify same-signature corruption exhaustively in this inventory pass.

searchLedger.rationale: The iteration target was exact write-path inventory; strategy schedules a dedicated blast-radius scan later.
searchLedger.deferredReason: Needs a path-aware JSON classifier rather than broad grep output.

focus: correctness determinism - Target 1 phase append parent description overwrite

findingDetails.title: Append-mode phase scaffolding deterministically overwrites the existing parent packet description.json
findingDetails.claim: Every successful create.sh --phase --phase-parent append invocation reaches the parent description generator with FEATURE_DIR set to the existing parent and --description set from the append request.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked parser alignment, positional versus EXPLICIT_NAME description handling, stale or missing parent description preconditions, generator state leakage, and the child generator path.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: External repair or backfill could also write metadata, but the reviewed append path is sufficient and unconditional under successful execution.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Append mode assigns FEATURE_DIR to PHASE_PARENT_RESOLVED and the parent generation block has no APPEND_TO_EXISTING_PARENT guard; child generation passes _child_path instead.
findingDetails.recommendation: Skip parent description generation in append mode or regenerate parent metadata from parent-owned content using a canonical specs-root base.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if an unseen runtime wrapper blocks parent generation in append mode, or if the deployed environment consistently lacks the dist generator or node execution.

checklist_evidence.reason: Target 1 determinism pass did not adjudicate checklist completion.

blast_radius_scan.reason: Exact grep found candidate metadata shapes; full path-aware classification is deferred.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Non-trivial multi-script metadata write path with an active P1 from iteration 1.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These files cover the parser, append-mode parent target assignment, parent and child generator invocations, canonical description construction, persistence semantics, and a read-only metadata signature probe.
omittedHighRiskTargets: Target 2 deriveStatus audit omitted because prompt says TARGET 1 ONLY.
Full path-aware repo-wide corruption classifier deferred to planned Target 1 blast-radius iteration.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Append mode always writes parent description metadata when the generator exists and succeeds.

searchActions.result: FEATURE_DIR is set to PHASE_PARENT_RESOLVED and parent generator runs without an append-mode guard.
Explicit description path builds canonical parent metadata from the append request and saves it.

searchLedger.rationale: The write path has no runtime branch that distinguishes new parent creation from append-to-existing parent before parent description generation.

searchLedger.invariant: Parser value consumption must not shift PHASE_PARENT or FEATURE_DESCRIPTION across parent and child paths.

searchActions.result: The parser stores phase-parent into PHASE_PARENT and positional args into ARGS; FEATURE_DESCRIPTION is derived once after parsing.

searchLedger.rationale: No shift-style parser bug is needed or visible for the parent overwrite.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct parser read shows stable variable assignment before phase mode.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A shared generator variable could leak parent state into child metadata or vice versa.

searchActions.result: CLI state is local to main; create.sh launches separate node commands for parent and child generation.

searchLedger.rationale: The deterministic parent overwrite occurs before child generation and does not depend on shared generator state.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No module-level mutable state participates in the reviewed CLI path.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The corruption might require a stale or missing parent description at scaffold time.

searchActions.result: Existing description only preserves memorySequence and memoryNameHistory; canonical fields come from the new explicit description payload.
Canonical fields overwrite existing canonical fields during merge.

searchLedger.rationale: Stale or missing metadata changes whether the bad payload overwrites or creates the file, not whether the parent write is attempted.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Canonical field construction ignores stale state for the fields involved in the corruption.

searchLedger.invariant: The child description generator must not be the direct writer of the parent description file.

searchActions.result: Child generation passes _child_path, not FEATURE_DIR.

searchLedger.rationale: The direct parent writer is the earlier parent generation block.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The child call target is the child folder path.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Existing repo metadata may contain same-signature parent description corruption.

searchActions.queryOrPath: specFolder basename-only and parentChain empty in description.json
searchActions.result: Found candidate basename-only specFolder and empty parentChain records; one nested candidate was read, but full classification requires path-aware JSON inspection.

searchLedger.rationale: The iteration target was determinism, not exhaustive blast-radius classification.
searchLedger.deferredReason: Requires a path-aware JSON classifier to separate legitimate roots, archived scratch records, and corrupted nested parent packets.

graphEvents.label: Append mode sets FEATURE_DIR to existing parent

focus: security blast-radius scan - Target 1 existing description.json corruption

findingDetails.title: Existing phase-parent metadata is already stored under basename-only identity in both metadata roots
findingDetails.claim: The 001-speckit-memory phase-parent packet already carries basename-only specFolder plus empty parentChain on disk despite living under system-speckit/028-memory-search-intelligence.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Ran a path-aware read-only classifier across both requested roots; classified 4918 description.json files and 512 phase-parent packets; directly read the only high-confidence candidate in both roots.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The two files may be mirrored roots and unrelated legacy drift exists elsewhere, but both persisted metadata files carry the exact identity-loss signature and remain metadata surfaces.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if both roots are proven non-indexed or if memory_search and graph traversal are proven to ignore description.json specFolder and parentChain for these packets.
findingDetails.scopeProof: 4918 description.json files scanned; 512 parent packets classified; 2 file candidates found, representing 1 logical packet duplicated under .opencode/specs and specs.
findingDetails.recommendation: After fixing create.sh, repair or regenerate both description.json copies for system-speckit/028-memory-search-intelligence/001-speckit-memory so specFolder and parentChain match the path identity.

checklist_evidence.reason: Iteration scope was Target 1 read-only metadata blast-radius scan, not checklist completion evidence.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Repo-wide read-only scan across both metadata roots and path-aware classification of phase-parent packets.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Prompt requested Target 1 blast-radius scan for parent-packet description.json corruption signatures only.
omittedHighRiskTargets: TARGET 2 deriveStatus completion-evidence audit omitted by explicit TARGET 1 ONLY focus.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Existing phase-parent description.json files may already have basename-only specFolder and empty parentChain from prior phase scaffolding.

searchActions.result: 4918 files scanned, 512 phase parents classified, 2 high-confidence files found.
specFolder is 001-speckit-memory and parentChain is [].

searchLedger.rationale: The affected file path implies system-speckit/028-memory-search-intelligence/001-speckit-memory, but persisted identity fields drop the parent path.

searchLedger.invariant: A parent packet description should summarize the parent purpose, not a child phase label or child-only scope.

searchActions.result: Parent title and purpose describe the Memory MCP phase parent and its 30 child folders.
Description also describes the phase parent rather than a child phase.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The high-confidence candidate has identity/lineage corruption, but its description body still aligns with the parent spec purpose.
searchLedger.rationale: No child-phase description overwrite was confirmed for the affected logical packet.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Other metadata drift may share discoverability risk but not the same create.sh parent overwrite signature.

searchActions.result: 332 related mismatch records observed, mostly absolute-root parentChain or legacy track-name mismatches.

searchLedger.deferredReason: Related drift is outside this Target 1 same-corruption count and needs its own data-quality audit before severity assignment.
searchLedger.rationale: The iteration target was same-corruption blast radius, so related historical drift was measured but not adjudicated as a new finding.

focus: maintainability minimal fix proposal - Target 1 only

findingDetails.title: Minimal fix: skip only existing-parent description regeneration in append mode
findingDetails.claim: Guard the parent description generation block so it does not run when APPEND_TO_EXISTING_PARENT=true; leave normal new-parent generation and child generation unchanged.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked child generation, phase-parent detection, and phase-classifier assumptions; none require parent description regeneration during append mode.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A generator-level hardening change could reject identity rewrites, but that would affect more callers than the observed append-mode defect.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if a runtime wrapper already prevents append-mode parent generation or if parent metadata regeneration is documented as required with parent-owned input.

findingDetails.title: Already-corrupted phase-parent metadata needs a scoped dry-run repair pass after the writer fix
findingDetails.claim: Fixing create.sh prevents future append-mode parent rewrites but does not repair the already-corrupted basename-only specFolder and empty parentChain found in iteration 3.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the affected packet was only a child-description overwrite and whether classifiers depend on parent description regeneration; both were ruled out.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Some historical metadata drift may be unrelated, so repair must target only same-signature candidates.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if the corrupted roots are proven unreachable by all scanners/indexers or an approved repair already exists.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Non-trivial Target 1 generated-metadata writer path with existing data corruption and repair proposal scope.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the confirmed writer, metadata generator, phase-parent detectors, phase classifier files named by scope, and the known corrupted packet from iteration 3.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A narrow append-mode guard around parent description generation fixes the defect without changing shared generator behavior.

searchActions.result: APPEND_TO_EXISTING_PARENT is set before FEATURE_DIR targets the parent; parent generation is separate from child generation.

searchLedger.rationale: The one-branch guard is the smallest fix matching the confirmed root cause.

searchLedger.invariant: The legitimate child description.json write must still run for each new child phase.

searchActions.result: Child block uses _child_path and is below the proposed parent-only guard.

searchLedger.rationale: The proposed guard is limited to the parent call and leaves the child loop untouched.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of separate parent and child generator calls.

searchLedger.invariant: Skipping parent description generation must not break phase-parent detection or phase classification assumptions.

searchActions.result: Phase-parent detection depends on direct child folders with spec.md or description.json, not parent description.json.
The named phase classifier classifies conversation phases, not spec-folder parent/child topology.

searchLedger.rationale: The fix does not change direct child folder creation or child metadata generation, and the named classifier is unrelated to folder topology.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified against classifier source assumptions.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Existing corrupted metadata persists after the writer fix and needs a separate scoped repair pass.

searchActions.result: specFolder is basename-only and parentChain is empty.
Parent spec still describes a phase-parent packet, so the same-signature corruption is identity/lineage metadata.

searchLedger.rationale: A preventive code fix alone leaves confirmed corrupted metadata on disk.

focus: correctness - Target 2 REQ-001..REQ-005 deriveStatus completion-evidence audit

findingDetails.title: Explicit complete statuses bypass the new completion-evidence gate
findingDetails.claim: deriveStatus can still produce a fresh complete result without completion_pct>=100 and without checking open tasks because normalized frontmatter/table status is returned before the patched no-checklist fallback runs.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked fallback branch, validator, rollout flag, and tests; fallback and validator are present but do not prevent the earlier status-precedence branch from returning complete.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Explicit status: Done may have been treated as authoritative completion evidence, but the phase requirements name completion_pct and open tasks as the evidence contract.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Spec amendment defining complete/done frontmatter as sufficient evidence, or code change routing complete-like frontmatter statuses through completion_pct/tasks evidence.

checklist_evidence.reason: This iteration audited spec.md REQ-001..REQ-005 directly.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Target 2 is a non-trivial spec-to-code audit across parser, validator, flag, bridge, and regression tests.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Files implement or test REQ-001..REQ-005 and the validate.sh bridge needed for REQ-004.

searchLedger.invariant: The no-checklist fallback must inspect completion_pct and open tasks before returning complete.

searchActions.result: fallback reads completionPct/tasks and returns complete only for completionPct>=100 with no open tasks
diff replaced the previous unconditional complete fallback with the evidence gate

searchLedger.rationale: The specific file-presence-only fallback was removed.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of the patched fallback.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A complete-like frontmatter status can bypass the new completion evidence gate.

searchActions.result: frontmatterStatus returns before the no-checklist completion evidence branch
test expects implementationSummaryStatus Done to derive complete without setting completion_pct

searchLedger.rationale: This bypass contradicts REQ-001/REQ-002 evidence-gating language and REQ-005 regression coverage expectations.

searchLedger.invariant: Stored complete graph metadata with absent or insufficient completion evidence must produce STATUS_COMPLETE_EVIDENCE_MISMATCH.

searchActions.result: validator pushes STATUS_COMPLETE_EVIDENCE_MISMATCH for absent pct, pct below 100, or open tasks
tests cover absent pct, low pct, open tasks, and genuine complete cases

searchLedger.rationale: The validator mismatch check exists and is tested for the required disagreement cases.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct code and test reads.

searchLedger.invariant: The new validator rule must be report/info by default and only fail strict when explicitly opted in.

searchActions.result: flag defaults false unless true/1
resolver treats the mismatch as blocking only when statusCompletionConsistencyEnforced is true; bridge passes the flag and exits only on error under strict

searchLedger.rationale: Default blocking behavior is not present for STATUS_COMPLETE_EVIDENCE_MISMATCH.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of flag, resolver, and bridge.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Tests may not fully pin the intended completion-evidence contract.

searchActions.result: tests cover fallback cases but also assert Done status derives complete without completion_pct
validator report/enforced modes are covered

searchLedger.rationale: REQ-005 is only partial because regression coverage currently pins the same explicit-status bypass as acceptable.

focus: correctness - Target 2 edge-case audit of deriveStatus completion_pct and no-open-tasks gate

findingDetails.title: Edge-case completion-evidence behavior lacks direct parser regression tests
findingDetails.claim: The shipped fallback handles the requested malformed pct, quoted pct, empty summary, zero-checkbox tasks and derive-only concurrency cases, but graph-metadata-schema.vitest.ts directly pins only numeric pct, absent pct, low pct and open-task cases.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Close if direct tests are added for quoted and non-numeric completion_pct, whitespace-only implementation-summary.md, comments-only tasks.md and concurrent derive-only calls, or if maintainers explicitly accept the current branch-level tests as sufficient.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Non-trivial shared status derivation parser edge-case audit.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The prompt constrained iteration 6 to Target 2 deriveStatus edge cases and asked whether graph-metadata-schema.vitest.ts already covers them.

searchLedger.invariant: A malformed or non-numeric completion_pct must not become fresh complete in the patched no-checklist fallback.

searchActions.result: Number(raw) must be finite or parseCompletionPct returns null.
Null pct returns preserved valid status or planned with review required, not fresh complete.

searchLedger.rationale: Fresh fallback does not coerce malformed pct to complete.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of parser branch.

searchLedger.invariant: A quoted numeric completion_pct should behave like a numeric completion_pct.

searchActions.result: Scalar extraction strips one layer of surrounding quotes.
Number(raw) converts quoted numeric text to a finite number after quote stripping.

searchLedger.rationale: Quoted numeric pct follows the same threshold check as numeric pct.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of extraction and numeric parsing.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A tasks.md file with no checkbox items may make no-open-tasks vacuously true.

searchActions.result: The helper intentionally treats zero checkbox items as no open tasks.
Requirements demand completion_pct evidence and no open tasks, not at least one checked task item.

searchLedger.rationale: The vacuous no-open result is explicit helper behavior and does not contradict the written acceptance criteria.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Spec lacks a positive-task-count requirement.

searchLedger.invariant: An empty or whitespace-only implementation-summary.md must not become fresh complete.

searchActions.result: No completion_pct scalar returns null.
Null pct maps to planned with review required for fresh derives.

searchLedger.rationale: Empty content lacks pct and follows the null-pct non-complete branch.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of parser branch.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Concurrent deriveStatus calls could race on shared state for the same folder.

searchActions.result: deriveGraphMetadata collects docs into local values and passes them to deriveStatus.
The module-level temp counter is write-only-path state, not deriveStatus state.

searchLedger.rationale: The reviewed derive path is read-only and local-state-only for same-folder concurrent derives.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No shared mutable deriveStatus state found.

searchLedger.invariant: Requested edge cases should be pinned if this behavior is relied on as completion evidence policy.

searchActions.result: Tests cover numeric 100, absent pct, below 100 and open tasks, not quoted or malformed pct, zero-byte summary, comments-only tasks or derive-only concurrency.
No direct parser edge-case tests found beyond unrelated malformed payload and writer interleaving coverage.

searchLedger.rationale: Behavior is acceptable but unpinned for the requested adversarial cases.

findingDetails.title: MCP validation orchestrator ignores the explicit status-completion enforcement flag

findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: If orchestrator is deprecated/unreachable, impact downgrades to parity cleanup.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if maintainers confirm orchestrator validation is not a supported entrypoint.

claimAdjudications.claim: Append-mode phase scaffolding overwrites an existing parent packet's description.json from child append input.
claimAdjudications.counterevidenceSought: child generator path and append-mode guards
claimAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: runtime wrapper prevents append-mode parent generation or built generator unavailable

claimAdjudications.claim: Existing phase-parent metadata is stored under basename-only identity in both metadata roots.
claimAdjudications.counterevidenceSought: parent spec body and duplicate-root interpretation
claimAdjudications.alternativeExplanation: duplicate root is an intentional mirror
claimAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: both roots proven unreachable or metadata contract ignores specFolder/parentChain

claimAdjudications.claim: Already-corrupted phase-parent metadata requires a scoped repair after the writer fix.
claimAdjudications.counterevidenceSought: minimal fix boundary and existing repair evidence
claimAdjudications.alternativeExplanation: separate repair workflow may already be planned
claimAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: approved repair workflow confirmed before ship or files proven non-indexed

claimAdjudications.claim: Explicit complete-like statuses bypass the new completion-evidence gate.
claimAdjudications.alternativeExplanation: explicit status intended as authoritative evidence
claimAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: spec amended or explicit statuses gated through completion evidence

claimAdjudications.claim: MCP validation orchestrator ignores status-completion enforcement flag.
claimAdjudications.counterevidenceSought: shell bridge wiring and resolver direct tests
claimAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: orchestrator path confirmed unreachable and supported entrypoints use shell bridge

claimAdjudications.claim: No orchestrator-level regression test covers the explicit status-completion enforcement flag.
claimAdjudications.counterevidenceSought: narrow test-tree grep for validateFolder/orchestrator/enforcement flag coverage
claimAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: orchestrator path confirmed unsupported or future validateFolder/orchestrator enforced-mode test added
